findings.evidence: Mathlib has `LinearMap.BilinForm.IsometryEquiv` with field `map_app' : ∀ n m, B₂ (toFun n) (toFun m) = B₁ n m` and `map_app`, `refl`, `symm`, `trans` at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/BilinearForm/IsometryEquiv.lean:40`, `:75`, `:80`, `:85`, `:93`; the PR repeats this at `SymplecticTransport.lean:115`, `:126`, `:144`, `:150`, `:158`.
findings.fix: Use `ω₁.toBilinForm.IsometryEquiv ω₂.toBilinForm` as the bundled symplectomorphism API, or make TauCeti's name a thin wrapper/bridge to it instead of a new Prop with parallel `refl`, `symm`, and `trans` lemmas.
findings.issue: `IsSymplectomorphism` and its pointwise/apply/refl/symm/trans API duplicate Mathlib's bilinear-form isometry equivalence API for exactly this preservation condition.

findings.evidence: The new definition duplicates the componentwise bilinear form and separating-left/right proof at `Prod.lean:214-239`; existing hits are `SymplecticForm.prod`/`prod_apply` at `Prod.lean:203`/`:209` and `SymplecticForm.rescale`/`rescale_apply` at `Rescale.lean:67`/`:92`, whose evaluations combine to `ω₁ p.1 q.1 - ω₂ p.2 q.2`.
findings.fix: Define it via the existing API, e.g. as `ω₁.prod (ω₂.rescale (-1) _)`, or move it to a file that can import `Rescale` and add only the desired `twistedProd_apply` simp lemma.
findings.issue: `twistedProd` reimplements the direct-sum construction and nondegeneracy proof instead of composing the existing `prod` with nonzero `rescale` on the second factor.

summary: The PR adds useful graph lemmas, but it introduces parallel symplectomorphism and twisted-product APIs instead of reusing existing bilinear-form isometry, product, and rescale machinery.
